1.name: Silvère
1.description: A young man of about seventeen, characterized by his vigorous appearance and youthful enthusiasm. He is involved in the republican insurgency and is deeply in love with Miette.

1.name: Miette
1.description: A thirteen-year-old girl on the cusp of womanhood, strong and full of life. She is in love with Silvère and is the daughter of a man who is in prison, which causes her some social stigma.

1.name: Chantegreil
1.description: Miette's father, who is in prison for killing a gendarme. He is remembered by some as an honest man, despite his crime.

1.name: Rébufat
1.description: Miette's uncle, with whom she lives. He is described as harsh and imposing hard work on Miette.

1.name: tante Dide
1.description: Silvère's aunt, who is mentioned as someone who would be worried if she saw Silvère taking his gun.

1.name: Burgat
1.description: A blacksmith from Alboise who joins the insurgents.

1.name: Antoine
1.description: Silvère's uncle, who is a worker and has taught Silvère to love the republic.

2.name: Pierre Rougon
2.description: Son of a peasant, ambitious and calculating, he becomes the head of the Rougon family and plays a significant role in the family's rise.

2.name: Adélaïde Fouque
2.description: Mother of Pierre Rougon, known for her erratic behavior and scandalous relationship with Macquart.

2.name: Macquart
2.description: A notorious and disreputable man, involved with Adélaïde, and father to her illegitimate children.

2.name: Antoine Macquart
2.description: Illegitimate son of Adélaïde and Macquart, known for his lazy and cunning nature.

2.name: Ursule Macquart
2.description: Illegitimate daughter of Adélaïde and Macquart, described as frail and resembling her mother.

2.name: Félicité Puech
2.description: Wife of Pierre Rougon, ambitious and cunning, she dreams of wealth and social status.

2.name: Eugène Rougon
2.description: Eldest son of Pierre and Félicité, ambitious with a desire for power, he moves to Paris sensing political change.

2.name: Aristide Rougon
2.description: Youngest son of Pierre and Félicité, known for his cunning and desire for quick wealth.

2.name: Pascal Rougon
2.description: Middle son of Pierre and Félicité, a doctor with a passion for science, living a modest life in Plassans.

2.name: Angèle Sicardot
2.description: Wife of Aristide Rougon, known for her appetite and love of flashy clothes.

2.name: Maxime Rougon
2.description: Son of Aristide and Angèle, sent to college by his grandmother Félicité.

2.name: Marthe Rougon
2.description: Daughter of Pierre and Félicité, married and living in Marseille.

2.name: Sidonie Rougon
2.description: Daughter of Pierre and Félicité, married and living in Paris.

2.name: Commandant Sicardot
2.description: Father of Angèle, a retired captain who provided a small dowry for his daughter.

2.name: Puech
2.description: Father of Félicité, a merchant whose business struggles lead to the marriage of his daughter to Pierre Rougon.

3.name: Félicité Rougon
3.description: Wife of Pierre Rougon, ambitious and cunning, she is deeply involved in the political intrigues of Plassans and seeks to secure her family's fortune.

3.name: Pierre Rougon
3.description: Husband of Félicité, a former oil merchant, he is involved in the political machinations of Plassans, aiming to capitalize on the political upheaval for personal gain.

3.name: Eugène Rougon
3.description: Son of Pierre and Félicité, he is involved in the Bonapartist movement and provides political guidance to his family from Paris.

3.name: Aristide Rougon
3.description: Son of Pierre and Félicité, a journalist with shifting political allegiances, he is initially a republican but is opportunistic and seeks to align with the winning side.

3.name: Pascal Rougon
3.description: Son of Pierre and Félicité, a doctor uninterested in politics, he is more focused on his medical practice and scientific interests.

3.name: M. de Carnavant
3.description: An elderly nobleman with ties to the Rougon family, he is involved in the political intrigues of Plassans and supports the legitimist cause.

3.name: M. Isidore Granoux
3.description: A retired almond merchant and member of the municipal council, he is a conservative and a regular at the Rougon's political gatherings.

3.name: M. Roudier
3.description: A wealthy retired bonnetier and Orléanist, he is influential in Plassans and a key figure in the conservative circle around the Rougons.

3.name: Commandant Sicardot
3.description: A retired military officer and father-in-law of Aristide, he is a staunch Bonapartist and supports the Rougons' political ambitions.

3.name: Vuillet
3.description: A bookseller and publisher of the Gazette de Plassans, he is a conservative and supports the clergy's interests in the political landscape of Plassans.

3.name: M. Garçonnet
3.description: The mayor of Plassans, a legitimist who is cautious about the political changes and maintains order in the town.

3.name: M. Peirotte
3.description: The current receveur particulier (tax collector) of Plassans, whose position Pierre Rougon aspires to take over.

3.name: Angèle
3.description: Wife of Aristide Rougon, she assists him in his journalistic endeavors and is involved in his political maneuverings.

4.name: Antoine Macquart
4.description: A lazy and envious man, brother of Pierre Rougon, who returns to Plassans after Napoleon's fall. He is embittered by his brother's success and becomes a fervent republican, hoping to take revenge on the Rougons.

4.name: Pierre Rougon
4.description: Antoine's brother, who has taken control of their mother's fortune. He is a conservative and is hiding during the republican uprising.

4.name: Adélaïde Fouque
4.description: Mother of Pierre and Antoine, living in isolation. She is detached from the world and has a mysterious past involving a braconnier named Macquart.

4.name: Félicité Rougon
4.description: Pierre Rougon's wife, who is concerned about the family's reputation and the scandal caused by Antoine.

4.name: Silvère Mouret
4.description: A young, idealistic republican and grandson of Adélaïde. He is involved in the uprising and is deeply influenced by his uncle Antoine's rhetoric.

4.name: Miette
4.description: A young girl who carries the red flag during the republican uprising. She is in a relationship with Silvère.

4.name: Jean Macquart
4.description: Son of Antoine Macquart, a hardworking young man who eventually leaves his father due to his laziness and exploitation.

4.name: Gervaise Macquart
4.description: Daughter of Antoine Macquart, who is physically weak and eventually leaves with her partner Lantier.

4.name: Joséphine Gavaudan
4.description: Antoine Macquart's wife, a hardworking woman who supports the family until her death.

4.name: Mouret
4.description: Silvère's father, who committed suicide after the death of his wife Ursule.

4.name: Ursule Mouret
4.description: Silvère's mother, who died of a slow illness, leaving her children orphaned.

4.name: François Mouret
4.description: Silvère's older brother, a diligent and sober young man who works for his uncle Pierre Rougon.

4.name: Marthe Rougon
4.description: Pierre Rougon's daughter, who marries François Mouret.

4.name: Pascal Rougon
4.description: Pierre Rougon's son, a doctor who helps the Macquart family during Antoine's illness.

4.name: Aristide Rougon
4.description: Pierre Rougon's son, a journalist with republican sympathies, but who keeps his distance from Antoine.

4.name: Eugène Rougon
4.description: Pierre Rougon's son, rumored to have a good position in Paris, but suspected by Antoine of being a police informant.

4.name: Maxime Rougon
4.description: Aristide's son, a young boy who is disrespectful towards Antoine.

4.name: Justin Rébufat
4.description: Miette's cousin, who harbors a deep hatred for her and insults her publicly.

4.name: M. Garçonnet
4.description: The mayor of Plassans, who tries to negotiate with the insurgents during their occupation of the town.

4.name: Commandant Sicardot
4.description: A military officer who wants to fight the insurgents but is overruled by the mayor.

4.name: Cassoute
4.description: A terrassier who follows Antoine Macquart's orders during the uprising.

4.name: Rengade
4.description: A gendarme who is injured by Silvère during the uprising.

5.name: Miette
5.description: A young girl, deeply in love with Silvère, who joins the insurrection and carries the red flag. She is strong-willed and determined, but ultimately meets a tragic end.

5.name: Silvère Mouret
5.description: A passionate young man and fervent republican, deeply in love with Miette. He is caught up in the insurrection and is devastated by Miette's death.

5.name: Tante Dide
5.description: Silvère's grandmother, who has a troubled past and experiences nervous crises. She is protective of Silvère and wary of the past repeating itself.

5.name: Justin
5.description: Miette's cousin, who torments her and is suspicious of her happiness.

5.name: Rébufat
5.description: Miette's uncle, who treats her harshly and exploits her labor.

5.name: Eulalie Chantegreil
5.description: Miette's aunt, who was protective of her before her death.

5.name: Chantegreil
5.description: Miette's father, a poacher sent to the penal colony for killing a gendarme.

5.name: Pascal Rougon
5.description: Silvère's cousin, a doctor who follows the insurrection out of curiosity and scientific interest.

5.name: Rengade
5.description: A gendarme injured by Silvère during the insurrection, who haunts Silvère's thoughts.

5.name: Sicardot
5.description: A military commander held prisoner by the insurgents, who is frustrated by the situation.

5.name: Garçonnet
5.description: The mayor of Orchères, held prisoner by the insurgents.

5.name: Peirotte
5.description: A government official held prisoner by the insurgents, who is killed during the battle.

5.name: Masson
5.description: A military officer leading the troops against the insurgents.

5.name: Blériot
5.description: The prefect of the department, accompanying the military forces against the insurgents.

6.name: Pierre Rougon
6.description: Ambitious and cunning head of the Rougon family, involved in political machinations to secure power in Plassans.

6.name: Félicité Rougon
6.description: Pierre Rougon's astute and manipulative wife, who plays a crucial role in orchestrating their political strategies.

6.name: Antoine Macquart
6.description: Pierre Rougon's half-brother, a schemer with a grudge against the Rougons, involved in republican activities.

6.name: Granoux
6.description: A timid and naive member of the municipal council, loyal to Rougon and known for his comical attempt to sound the tocsin with a hammer.

6.name: Roudier
6.description: A former bonnetier and commander of the National Guard in Plassans, supportive of Rougon's cause.

6.name: Aristide Rougon
6.description: Pierre and Félicité's son, initially hesitant but ultimately aligns with his parents' political ambitions.

6.name: Eugène Rougon
6.description: Pierre and Félicité's son, who provides crucial political information from Paris, influencing his parents' actions.

6.name: Vuillet
6.description: A cunning and opportunistic bookseller who manipulates information for personal gain.

6.name: M. de Blériot
6.description: The prefect of the department, who arrives with the military to restore order in Plassans.

6.name: Colonel Masson
6.description: A military officer who arrives with the prefect to secure Plassans.

6.name: M. Garçonnet
6.description: The mayor of Plassans, whose office becomes a focal point during the political turmoil.

6.name: Tante Dide
6.description: The mother of Antoine Macquart, who remains detached from the political events.

6.name: Silvère Mouret
6.description: A young republican insurgent related to the Rougons, involved in the uprising.

6.name: Miette
6.description: Silvère's companion, known for her distinctive red attire during the insurrection.

6.name: M. de Carnavant
6.description: An aristocrat with ties to the Rougons, observing the political events with interest.

7.name: Pierre Rougon
7.description: Ambitious head of the Rougon family, eagerly awaiting rewards for his political loyalty.

7.name: Félicité Rougon
7.description: Pierre Rougon's wife, deeply invested in her family's social ascent.

7.name: Eugène Rougon
7.description: Pierre and Félicité's son, involved in political machinations in Paris.

7.name: Aristide Rougon
7.description: Pierre and Félicité's son, opportunistic and eager to advance his career in Paris.

7.name: Pascal Rougon
7.description: Pierre and Félicité's son, a doctor concerned with the well-being of his family.

7.name: Silvère Mouret
7.description: Young republican insurgent, deeply in love with Miette, ultimately executed.

7.name: Antoine Macquart
7.description: Pierre Rougon's brother, a discontented and scheming character.

7.name: Tante Dide
7.description: Mother of Pierre Rougon and Antoine Macquart, suffers from a mental breakdown.

7.name: Miette Chantegreil
7.description: Silvère's beloved, who has died, leaving him in despair.

7.name: M. Garçonnet
7.description: The mayor of Plassans, hosting a dinner for the prefect and the colonel.

7.name: Sicardot
7.description: A character who is envious of Pierre Rougon's new decoration.

7.name: Granoux
7.description: A member of the bourgeoisie, part of the Rougon's social circle.

7.name: Roudier
7.description: A wealthy bourgeois, part of the Rougon's social circle.

7.name: Vuillet
7.description: A bookseller, part of the Rougon's social circle.

7.name: M. Peirotte
7.description: The deceased former tax collector, whose position Pierre Rougon hopes to obtain.

7.name: Rengade
7.description: A gendarme seeking revenge on Silvère for blinding him.

7.name: Mourgue
7.description: A peasant from Poujols, executed alongside Silvère.

7.name: Justin
7.description: A young boy who takes pleasure in witnessing Silvère's execution.

7.name: M. de Blériot
7.description: A character whom Pierre Rougon plans to visit for assistance.

7.name: M. de Carnavant
7.description: A noble who declines the Rougon's dinner invitation, preferring to distance himself.
